name: Sourdough Raisin-Oat Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Lukewarm evaporated milk            1 teaspoon Ground allspice
1 1/2 cups Sourdough starter                   2 cups Unprocessed rolled oats
recipeInstructions: Raisins are tossed lightly with a little flour. In a large warmed bowl, combine milk, starter, molasses, honey, butter, salt and allspice. Gradually add oats, a small amount at a time, mixing well, then stir in raisins. Beat in flour, 1/2 cup at a time, to form a stiff dough. Turn out onto floured surface and knead 5-7 minutes, adding additional flour only as needed to prevent sticking. Form into smooth ball, place in oiled bowl, turn to coat all surfaces, cover with dampened towel and let rise in warm place at least 2 hours or until double in bulk. Punch down, turn out onto board and knead briefly. Cover with towel and let rise 10 minutes. Divide dough into 2 equal portions, form into loaves and place in 2 #2 loaf pans. Cover with towel and let rise in warm place 1-1/2 hours or until almost double in size. Bake at 375°F for 35-40 minutes or until bread tests done. Turn out onto wire rack, turn right side up and cool.
